title: Tag
description: Tag renders compact labels for metadata, status, selection chips, and removable tokens. It supports semantic variants, appearances, optional icons, secondary text, selectable state, and dismiss actions.
intro: Use `ui-tag` for short labels that add context without taking much space: status chips, filter tokens, categories, owner metadata, or removable selections. Tags can be purely informational, selectable like filter chips, or dismissible like lightweight tokens.

featureSections.title: Basic metadata tags
featureSections.description: Use tags for short metadata and lightweight labels. Secondary text is useful for compact paired values such as owner plus name or label plus code.

featureSections.title: Appearance and variant
featureSections.description: Appearance changes visual emphasis, while variant carries semantic meaning. Use quieter styles for metadata and stronger styles for status or priority.

featureSections.title: Size and shape
featureSections.description: Size helps tags fit dense toolbars or larger cards, while shape changes how soft or structural the chip feels in the surrounding layout.

featureSections.title: Selectable filter chips
featureSections.description: Tags become interactive chips when `selectable` is enabled. This works well for lightweight filter states that should not require full checkbox controls.

featureSections.title: Dismissible tokens
featureSections.description: Dismissible tags fit selected values, assigned labels, and quick-edit token groups where the user should be able to remove items one by one.

featureSections.title: Metadata panel composition
featureSections.description: A stronger end-to-end example: tags grouped inside a richer panel to communicate project state, ownership, and cross-system references.

accessibility.title: Informational vs interactive
accessibility.body: Plain informational tags are not focusable by default. When `selectable` is enabled, the tag becomes keyboard-focusable and exposes button-like interaction with `aria-selected`.

accessibility.title: Keyboard
accessibility.body:
| Key | Action |
| --- | --- |
| Tab | Moves focus to selectable or dismissible tags only. |
| Enter / Space | Toggles a selectable tag. |
| Enter / Space on dismiss button | Removes the tag when dismissible. |

accessibility.title: Dismiss semantics
accessibility.body: Dismissible tags render a dedicated button with an accessible label like `Remove Design`. The dismiss button is hidden when the tag is disabled, so users do not encounter an unavailable removal control.

apiSections.title: TagComponent - ui-tag
apiSections.lead: Standalone tag component for metadata, selection chips, and removable tokens. It can stay purely informational or become interactive through selectable and dismissible states.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Optional secondary line. Rendered only for medium size.
Makes the tag interactive and toggles `selected` on click or keyboard activation.
Shows a remove button inside the tag.
Optional accessible name override for the main tag element.

blocks.title: Outputs
rows: Emits when a selectable tag is activated.
Emits when the dismiss button is activated.

paragraphs: Secondary text is intentionally limited to medium size so the component stays compact in denser layouts.
Informational tags are not focusable by default. If users need to interact with a tag, prefer `selectable`, `dismissible`, or both so the semantics stay explicit.
blocks.title: Notes
